evals.prompt: We sell a B2B SaaS platform — two products: DataSync (data integration, $20-80k deals) and DataVault (data warehousing, $40-150k deals). Each has SMB and enterprise tiers. We have 12 reps using HubSpot, and right now everyone builds proposals from scratch in Google Docs — they're wildly inconsistent, take 2-3 hours each, and pricing is presented differently every time. We want to move to Qwilr templates that auto-populate from HubSpot with company name, contact info, deal amount, and product tier. Help me design the template system.
evals.expected_output: A template strategy recommending 3-4 templates (product x segment matrix, capped sensibly), with detailed blueprints for each including section structure, block types, fixed vs. variable sections, copy frameworks for variable sections, and a token design for CRM auto-population.

assertions.name: has_template_strategy
assertions.description: Recommends how many templates to create and why, based on the 2-product x 2-segment matrix

assertions.name: has_blueprints
assertions.description: Provides detailed section-by-section blueprints for each template

assertions.name: has_copy_frameworks
assertions.description: Includes fill-in-the-blank frameworks for variable sections like executive summary

assertions.name: has_token_design
assertions.description: Defines substitution tokens mapped to CRM fields

evals.prompt: I need to create vertical-specific Qwilr templates for our sales team. We sell an enterprise compliance automation platform ($50-200k deals) to healthcare, financial services, and education. We have 8 reps and currently use one generic template that nobody likes. Healthcare needs HIPAA language and we have 3 healthcare case studies. Financial services needs SOC 2 and PCI-DSS sections and we have 2 finserv case studies. Education needs FERPA compliance and budget-friendly pricing tiers — we have 1 education case study and are working on a second. Each vertical also has different ROI metrics — healthcare focuses on audit time savings, finserv on risk reduction, education on enrollment compliance rates. Help me design templates with a shared base, vertical-specific sections, and a case study strategy for all three verticals.
evals.expected_output: A template design with a shared base structure and vertical-specific conditional sections for compliance, case studies, and industry-specific language. Should include token design for industry-specific fields and guidance on managing the case study library.

assertions.name: has_shared_base
assertions.description: Designs a shared template base with conditional vertical-specific sections

assertions.name: has_vertical_customization
assertions.description: Addresses specific needs per vertical (healthcare/HIPAA, financial services/SOC 2/PCI-DSS, education/FERPA)

assertions.name: has_case_study_strategy
assertions.description: Recommends how to organize and select case studies per vertical

evals.prompt: Design tokens for our Qwilr templates so we can auto-generate proposals from Salesforce. We need company info, deal info, and rep info to auto-populate.
evals.expected_output: A complete token mapping from Salesforce fields to Qwilr template tokens, including standard tokens, custom tokens, guidelines for which tokens should be auto vs. manual, and fallback values for empty fields.

assertions.name: has_salesforce_mapping
assertions.description: Maps specific Salesforce fields (Account, Opportunity, Contact, User) to Qwilr tokens

assertions.name: has_auto_vs_manual
assertions.description: Distinguishes which tokens should auto-populate vs. require manual input

assertions.name: has_fallback_guidance
assertions.description: Addresses what happens when CRM fields are empty
